Harmonized Name: Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity
Source Label / Description: The ratio of the volume of air that can be forcibly exhaled from the lungs in the first second of a forced exhalation (FEV1) to the total volume of air that can be forcibly exhaled from the lungs (FVC).
Summary: The FEV1/FVC ratio is a key measure in diagnosing obstructive and restrictive lung disease. It represents the proportion of a person's vital capacity that they are able to exhale in the first second of forced expiration. The LOINC code 19926-5 is used to represent this concept in a standardized manner.
